title: AI's REAL Strength: Greenfield Projects, Not Legacy Code! #shorts
summary: AI performs well on greenfield projects but struggles integrating into legacy systems due to poor documentation and inherent complexity. This reflects a structural challenge in where AI can be most effectively deployed in software development.
writeup: This short from Cyril Imhof argues that AI coding tools excel on greenfield projects but struggle to integrate into legacy systems, where poor documentation and inherent complexity limit their effectiveness. The distinction matters because it points to a structural constraint on where AI can be deployed productively in software development, rather than a uniform capability across all codebases. The takeaway: new builds favor AI assistance, while legacy migration remains a harder, less tractable target.
